title: NOC 32103 – Respiratory Therapists, Clinical Perfusionists, and Cardiopulmonary Technologists | GTR Immigration
description: Respiratory Therapists, Clinical Perfusionists, and Cardiopulmonary Technologists under NOC 32103 are highly valued healthcare professionals across Canada. Demand has increased significantly due to respiratory care expansion, cardiac surgery advancements, and long-term pulmonary disease management. As a TEER 2 occupation, NOC 32103 offers strong eligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs, making it a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3210 – Technical occupations in therapy and assessmentNOC Code32103

duties: Conduct arterial blood gas and cardiopulmonary function testing
Operate and monitor ventilators and respiratory equipment
Perform airway management and intubation assistance
Assist in patient transport and emergency response
Operate heart-lung machines during cardiac surgery
Administer blood products and medications during surgery
Monitor vital signs during cardiopulmonary procedures
Conduct pulmonary and cardiac stress tests
Provide patient care during diagnostic testing
employment_requirements: Completion of a three-year college or university program in respiratory therapy
Licensing required in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, and Alberta
National certification available in Prince Edward Island, British Columbia, Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut
Completion of respiratory therapy or nursing program
Clinical perfusion program with clinical training
Certification with Canadian Society of Clinical Perfusion required
Two-year allied health diploma or relevant university degree
Registration with Canadian Association of Cardio-pulmonary Technologists may be required
Internationally educated professionals must complete credential recognition and provincial registration before practice.
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range from $50,100 to $92,400, depending on specialization, province, and level of certification.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32103 remains positive nationwide. Demand drivers include:
Growth in cardiac surgery and cardiovascular services
Aging population with chronic pulmonary conditions
Expansion of sleep and home respiratory therapy programs
Professionals with advanced certifications and Canadian registration have strong employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 32103 are employed in:
This positive employment balance highlights sustained demand. Strong employment markets include Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Manitoba, Quebec, and Saskatchewan.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32103?
Professionals in this occupation benefit from:
Strong job security in public healthcare systems
Access to universal healthcare and settlement benefits
Cardiopulmonary and respiratory care remain critical to Canada’s healthcare infrastructure.
pathways: If you are a qualified Respiratory Therapist, Clinical Perfusionist, or Cardiopulmonary Technologist, NOC 32103 offers a high-demand immigration pathway to Canada.
Eligibility assessment under Express Entry and PNP
